raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.0,"R1.2":0.0,"R1.3":0.3,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.0,"R1.7":1.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.7":"The delegation 'Check the citations' is a pure citation-and-scholarship task: verify accuracy of the sole explicit reference (Eyring et al., 2016 for CMIP6), identify uncited empirical claims (the 20–40% adaptation-gap figure, the ACS calibration for Miami-Dade and Charleston, the 'bounded rationality / social learning' attribution), audit coverage of prior generative-agent work backing the 'interactive simulacra' architecture allusion, and evaluate whether the 'first agent-based simulation using LLM-driven agents' novelty claim is supported by adequate coverage of related work."},"clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":""}

rationale.R1.7: The delegation 'Check the citations' is a pure citation-and-scholarship task: verify accuracy of the sole explicit reference (Eyring et al., 2016 for CMIP6), identify uncited empirical claims (the 20–40% adaptation-gap figure, the ACS calibration for Miami-Dade and Charleston, the 'bounded rationality / social learning' attribution), audit coverage of prior generative-agent work backing the 'interactive simulacra' architecture allusion, and evaluate whether the 'first agent-based simulation using LLM-driven agents' novelty claim is supported by adequate coverage of related work.
